what_it_does: This file is a JSON allocation batch that lists six code/design substrate entries for the claude-ds project, each identified by project name, relative path, and an absolute filesystem root.
observations: All six entries share the same project ('claude-ds') and root ('/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds')
Paths include three JS templates, one HTML template, and two CSS token files
Two entries share the basename 'support.js' but live in different template directories ('capital-raise' and 'pitch-deck')
The file declares no code symbols ('symbols[] = []')
The directory path suggests this belongs to wave-002, alloc batch-037 of an interpretive ledger
standouts: Mix of pitch-deck/capital-raise templates and canvas/controls tokens in one batch
Duplicate 'support.js' filenames under different template folders
Absolute local root path embedded in each entry
No symbol metadata despite listing code/design substrates
concerns: Absolute root path '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds' is machine-local and may not port
Two files named 'support.js' could be mistaken for duplicates without directory context
No stated criteria for why these six files were grouped into batch-037
No versioning, timestamp, or author metadata
notes: A machine-local allocation batch that inventories a small mixed set of claude-ds templates and token CSS files for downstream interpretation.
questions: What selection criteria placed these six specific files into batch-037?
What does 'wave-002' and 'alloc' signify in the broader ledger workflow?
Are the two 'support.js' files independent helpers or intended to share logic?
Who consumes this batch and what processing step follows?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the path frames it as an interpretive allocation batch, and the content is exactly a list of design substrates allocated to that batch.
intention_signals: It appears to enumerate a curated slice of claude-ds design substrates so an interpretive or fusion process can locate and ingest them together.
novelty: It records an absolute root per entry rather than relative paths, and it groups heterogeneous file types (JS, HTML, CSS) under a single allocation batch.
contribution: It contributes a scoped, typed manifest of claude-ds template and token files with their filesystem root, giving a fused system a concrete ingestion list for this batch.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six claude-ds design substrates—pitch-deck/capital-raise templates and canvas/controls tokens—each with project, relative path, and absolute root.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to catalog a specific wave/batch of claude-ds design substrates for downstream interpretive or fusion processing.
